metadata.summary: Automate transcript processing and cross-interview theme extraction using AI transcription and Claude agents with quote-grounded theme extraction.

content.title: AI-Assisted Interview Analysis
content.summary: Use AI to automate transcript processing and cross-interview theme extraction. A local or cloud transcription model (Whisper, AssemblyAI) converts recordings to text, then a Claude agent segments speakers, extracts themes supported by verbatim quotes, scores sentiment, and produces a cross-interview pattern report. The human researcher reviews and interprets; the agent handles volume.

why.p: Manual transcription and analysis of 5+ interview sessions takes days. AI transcription runs in minutes with 90%+ accuracy on clear audio, and theme extraction with forced quote-grounding eliminates unsupported assertions. The result: researchers spend time on interpretation, not mechanical coding.

list.item: Processing 5+ interview recordings where manual transcription/analysis would take days
Extracting cross-interview themes for affinity diagramming or synthesis reporting
Generating first-pass summaries of individual sessions before researcher review
Running sentiment scoring on large transcript corpora to prioritize review order
Producing pattern reports as input for product team debriefs
Single sessions where a researcher can take notes live — no meaningful speed gain
Moderated usability studies where in-session facilitator judgment is the core deliverable
Studies involving sensitive disclosures (health, legal) without explicit AI-processing consent
High-stakes research where hesitation, emotional cues, or nonverbal behavior are critical — AI is text-only
Any pipeline where AI output goes to stakeholders without researcher review

section.name: tool-stack
section.p: Transcription services, tool comparison by use case, accuracy benchmarks, and data residency considerations for AI-assisted interview analysis.

rule.statement: Use local transcription (Whisper/faster-whisper) for sensitive participant data before considering cloud services.
rule.rationale: Most SaaS tools (Looppanel, Dovetail, Insight7) process data on US servers. Verify GDPR/CCPA compliance before uploading participant data.

list.item: Looppanel — UX research focus, 17 languages, 90%+ accuracy, REST API
Dovetail — research repository and pattern detection; insights exportable, no full transcript API
Insight7 — bulk interview analysis, sentiment, REST API for upload + theme extraction
UserBit — 50+ languages, speaker identification, no public API
AssemblyAI — best transcription API; sentiment, entities, chapters, speaker labels
Otter.ai — real-time transcription, REST API for transcript export only
Fireflies.ai — meeting transcription + action items, REST API
Whisper (OpenAI OSS) — local transcription, no built-in speaker diarization
faster-whisper — 4x faster Whisper variant, GPU-optional
pyannote-audio — speaker diarization (who spoke when)
State-of-the-art transcription on good audio: up to 99%
Speaker identification: good reliability, degrades with 3+ participants or similar vocal qualities
Sentiment analysis: improving but requires human review — poor on sarcasm and cultural expressions
AI-only captions: 80–90% accuracy — does not meet the 99% legal requirement
AI + human review captions: 95–99% accuracy
Transcription: hours → minutes
Summarization: hours → seconds
Theme extraction: days → hours
Cross-interview patterns: weeks → hours

rule.statement: Never use AI for behavioral annotation — current tools are text-only.
rule.rationale: AI cannot observe nonverbal interactions, hesitation timing, or emotional cues from video. Only text content is processed.

list.item: Cannot observe or facilitate moderated studies
Cannot understand nonverbal interactions
Limited behavioral analysis beyond text
Requires human interpretation of insights

section.name: analysis-workflow
section.p: Step-by-step pipeline from raw recording to validated theme report, including quote-grounding rules, confidence annotation, and agent subagent selection.

list.item: 1. Upload recording to transcription service or run Whisper locally
2. Export transcript text; review speaker diarization for 3+ participant sessions (100%)
3. Feed transcript(s) to Claude agent with discussion guide structure as context
4. Agent segments by speaker, extracts themes with quotes, scores sentiment
5. Agent produces cross-interview pattern report
6. Researcher reviews: accepts, rejects, or re-codes themes
7. Annotate confidence: High (3+ participants), Medium (2), Low (1/agent inference)

rule.statement: Reject any theme the agent cannot support with 2+ direct participant quotes from different transcripts.
rule.rationale: Agents generate plausible-sounding themes not grounded in transcript text. Exact quote evidence is the only reliable signal.

rule.statement: Preserve exact participant quotes — never accept paraphrased evidence.
rule.rationale: Paraphrasing introduces researcher interpretation at the analysis stage; the agent should lift verbatim text only.

rule.statement: Mark any demographic inference not directly stated by the participant as [INFERRED — needs validation].
rule.rationale: Agents hallucinate participant demographics from thin cues (e.g., "she mentioned her commute" → "urban professional").

rule.statement: Chunk long transcripts (90min+) by discussion guide section, not by arbitrary token count.
rule.rationale: Long transcripts exceed Claude's context window when combined. Guide sections are semantic boundaries; arbitrary chunking splits mid-thought and corrupts theme extraction.

list.item: haiku — transcript segmentation, quote extraction, Likert-scale sentiment scoring at volume
sonnet — theme synthesis, cross-interview pattern detection, insight gap identification, final report generation

section.name: anti-patterns
section.p: Common agent failures in interview analysis: hallucinated themes, speaker misattribution, sentiment errors, and compliance traps.

antipattern.description: Publishing AI analysis output directly to stakeholders without researcher review.
antipattern.reason: AI analysis output is a draft, not a finding. Agents produce plausible-sounding themes that may not be grounded in the actual transcript. Researcher review is the only quality gate.

antipattern.description: Accepting themes where the agent cannot provide a direct verbatim quote.
antipattern.reason: Agents generate unsupported themes confidently. The quote requirement forces the agent to ground outputs in evidence and makes hallucination visible.

antipattern.description: Skipping speaker diarization review for sessions with 3+ participants.
antipattern.reason: Speaker identification degrades with similar vocal qualities. Misattributed quotes corrupt theme analysis downstream — a minority participant's view gets credited to the majority.

antipattern.description: Applying sentiment analysis findings without contextualizing researcher tone or framing effects.
antipattern.reason: AI sentiment scoring does not account for how researcher phrasing influenced participant responses. Negative sentiment on a leading question is not a product finding.

antipattern.description: Uploading sensitive participant data (health, legal, financial disclosures) to SaaS transcription tools without verifying consent and data residency.
antipattern.reason: GDPR/CCPA requires AI processing disclosure in participant consent forms. Most SaaS tools process on US servers — non-compliant for EU participants without appropriate lawful basis.

antipattern.description: Chunking long transcripts by token count rather than discussion guide section.
antipattern.reason: Arbitrary token boundaries split mid-thought, causing the agent to lose context for theme attribution. Section-based chunking preserves semantic coherence.

antipattern.description: Treating agent-inferred participant demographics as confirmed facts.
antipattern.reason: Agents hallucinate persona characteristics from thin linguistic cues. Any demographic not explicitly stated by the participant must be flagged as inferred and validated separately.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Claude agents can perform full transcript analysis pipelines: given raw transcript text, the agent (1) segments by speaker, (2) extracts themes with supporting quotes, (3) scores sentiment per segment, (4) produces a cross-interview pattern report. The human uploads recordings to a transcription service (Looppanel, Otter.ai), exports transcript text, feeds to the agent, and then reviews agent output — accepting, rejecting, or re-coding themes. The agent handles volume; the researcher handles interpretation quality.

rule.statement: Transcription accuracy drops sharply with accents, background noise, technical jargon, and cross-talk — always review low-confidence segments.
rule.rationale: Transcription accuracy reflects input audio quality. Poor audio requires manual spot-checking to catch service errors before theme extraction.

rule.statement: Speaker identification fails with more than 3 participants or when speakers have similar vocal qualities.
rule.rationale: Diarization models rely on vocal distinctiveness. When participants sound similar or overlap frequently, manual speaker label correction is required.

rule.statement: Theme extraction reflects training data bias — minority opinions in transcripts are systematically underweighted.
rule.rationale: AI models amplify patterns present in training data. Edge cases and minority views may be suppressed in theme extraction — deliberate researcher attention is required.

rule.statement: Sentiment analysis is poor on sarcasm, understatement, and culturally specific expressions.
rule.rationale: Sentiment models trained on English social media struggle with nuanced emotional expression. Cross-cultural and sarcastic speech require human interpretation.

rule.statement: AI summaries compress nuance; key insight details (timing, specific wording) are often lost.
rule.rationale: Summarization introduces abstraction. Critical context (e.g., "customer got frustrated midway through task") may be stripped.

rule.statement: None of the current tools handle video + behavioral annotation — text only.
rule.rationale: Transcription services and AI agents work from text. Nonverbal analysis requires separate observation and annotation by a human or specialized video analysis tool.

rule.statement: Data residency: most SaaS tools (Looppanel, Dovetail, Insight7) process data on US servers — verify compliance before uploading participant data.
rule.rationale: GDPR and CCPA require localization or equivalent safeguards. US-based processing is non-compliant for EU participants without a data processing agreement.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: Recommended command-line tools and services for interview transcription, speaker diarization, and theme extraction:

list.item: whisper (OpenAI) — Local transcription — no data leaves machine (pip install openai-whisper)
faster-whisper — 4x faster Whisper variant, GPU-optional (pip install faster-whisper)
pyannote-audio — Speaker diarization (who spoke when) (pip install pyannote.audio)
anthropic Python SDK — Transcript theme extraction via Claude (pip install anthropic)
assemblyai Python SDK — Cloud transcription + speaker labels + sentiment (pip install assemblyai)
Looppanel — SaaS, UX research focus; 17 languages; REST API for transcript + tags
Dovetail — SaaS, Research repo; no full transcript API; insights exportable
Insight7 — SaaS, Bulk analysis; REST API for upload + theme extraction
UserBit — SaaS, 50+ languages; no public API
AssemblyAI — SaaS, Best transcription API; sentiment + entities + chapters
Otter.ai — SaaS, REST API for transcript export; no analysis API
Fireflies.ai — SaaS, Meeting transcription + action items; REST API
Descript — SaaS, Video + transcript editing; no analysis API
Whisper (OpenAI OSS) — OSS, Local transcription; no speaker diarization built-in
Always use local transcription (Whisper/faster-whisper) for sensitive participant data before considering cloud services
Review 100% of speaker diarization output for 3+ participant sessions — misattribution corrupts theme analysis
Set a minimum quote threshold: reject any theme the agent cannot support with 2+ direct participant quotes
Structure your discussion guide with labeled sections (e.g., "## Section 3: Task Completion") — agent theme extraction aligns to guide structure when present
Produce a "confidence annotation" on the final report: mark each theme as High (3+ participants), Medium (2 participants), Low (1 participant / agent inference)
Never automate insight delivery to product teams without researcher review — AI analysis output is a draft, not a finding

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.p: Example system and user prompts for Claude agent-based interview analysis:

example.title: Multi-Interview Theme Extraction
example.code: <system> You are a UX research analyst. Analyze interview transcripts systematically. Rules: (1) extract only themes with 2+ supporting quotes from different participants, (2) flag themes appearing in only 1 transcript as "weak signal", (3) preserve exact participant quotes — never paraphrase, (4) note where participants hesitate or contradict themselves. </system> <human> Analyze these transcripts from [N] participants interviewed about [topic]. Discussion guide topics: [list]. Output: 1. Top themes (≥2 participants) with evidence quotes 2. Weak signals (1 participant) worth noting 3. Contradictions or outliers 4. Gaps: discussion guide questions with no usable responses </human>

example.title: Single-Interview Structured Summary
example.code: Analyze this single interview transcript [paste]. Output structured summary: (1) participant profile (inferred from responses), (2) key pain points mentioned with direct quotes, (3) positive reactions with quotes, (4) unresolved questions (things participant was unclear on), (5) follow-up questions for next session. Max 400 words.

template.purpose: Full audio-to-themes pipeline using faster-whisper and Claude; demonstrates local transcription and agent-based theme extraction
Claude system+user prompt for cross-interview theme analysis with quote-grounding constraint
Prompt for structured single-session summary with participant profile and follow-up questions
